quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 7, Grade 11 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Genetics: Genotype Analysis, Gamete Formation, and Probability
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Genotype Notation, Gamete Combinations, Dihybrid Cross Probabilities, Monohybrid Cross Outcomes, Carrier Identification, DNA Complementarity

questions.question: Genetic traits of seeds are noted as follows: L = long, l = short; W = wrinkled, w = smooth; Y = yellow, y = white; R = ribbed, r = grooved. Which of the following is the genotype for a short, wrinkled, yellow, grooved seed?
options: llWwyyrr
LLWWyYRr
LlWwYYRr
llWwYYrr
questions.explanation: Short requires ll (homozygous recessive). Wrinkled requires at least one W (Ww or WW). Yellow requires at least one Y (Yy or YY). Grooved requires rr (homozygous recessive). Option D: llWwYYrr matches all requirements.

questions.question: Mendel discovered principles of inheritance because he:
options: Observed simultaneously all of the many characteristics in which the parents differed.
Believed that the hereditary characteristics of two individuals became thoroughly blended in the offspring.
Ignored all characteristics except a few markedly contrasting ones in which he studied.
Studied only the offspring obtained from a single mating.
questions.explanation: Mendel's success came from focusing on a few discrete, contrasting traits (like tall/short, yellow/green) rather than trying to study all traits at once. This allowed him to see clear inheritance patterns.

questions.question: Two heterozygotes for seed shape (Rr) and color (Yy) are crossed. Probability of offspring being round and yellow (R–Y–) is:
questions.explanation: RrYy × RrYy is a dihybrid cross. Probability of R– = 3/4, Y– = 3/4. Combined probability = 3/4 × 3/4 = 9/16 for round and yellow phenotype.

questions.question: Cross between AaBb × aabb produces offspring with probability of being AaBb:
questions.explanation: Probability of Aa from Aa × aa = 1/2. Probability of Bb from Bb × bb = 1/2. Combined probability = 1/2 × 1/2 = 1/4 for AaBb genotype.

questions.question: In pea plants, round (R) is dominant over wrinkled (r), yellow (Y) over green (y). Cross RrYy × RrYy. Probability of wrinkled green offspring is:
questions.explanation: Wrinkled = rr (1/4 probability). Green = yy (1/4 probability). Combined probability = 1/4 × 1/4 = 1/16 for rryy (wrinkled green).

questions.question: An individual has the genotype BBFF. How many different kinds of gametes can this individual produce?
questions.explanation: BBFF is homozygous for both genes. Only one gamete type is possible: BF. All gametes are identical.

questions.question: An individual has the genotype ddffMm. How many different kinds of gametes can this individual produce?
questions.explanation: dd and ff are homozygous, so they contribute only d and f. Mm is heterozygous, producing M and m. Gametes: dfM and dfm → 2 types.

questions.question: An individual has the genotype BbffMm. How many different kinds of gametes can this individual produce?
questions.explanation: Bb (2 possibilities) × ff (1 possibility) × Mm (2 possibilities) = 2 × 1 × 2 = 4 gamete types: BfM, Bfm, bfM, bfm.

questions.question: Carriers of the colour-blindness trait include:
options: Men who are heterozygous for the trait.
Men who are homozygous for the trait.
Women who are heterozygous for the trait.
Women who are homozygous for the trait.
questions.explanation: Color blindness is X-linked recessive. Women can be carriers if heterozygous (XᴺXᶜ). Men cannot be carriers because they have only one X chromosome—they are either affected (XᶜY) or normal (XᴺY).

questions.question: A strand of DNA with the sequence A A C T T G will have a complimentary strand with the following sequence:
options: CCAGGT
AACTTG
TTCAAG
TTGAAC
questions.explanation: DNA base pairing rules: A pairs with T, C pairs with G. Original: A A C T T G. Complementary: T T G A A C. Option D (TTGAAC) matches.

questions.question: Normal human eggs have:
options: 22 autosomes and an X chromosome.
22 autosomes and a Y chromosome.
23 autosomes.
46 chromosomes.
questions.explanation: Human eggs are haploid: 22 autosomes + 1 sex chromosome (always X). Sperm can have 22 + X or 22 + Y.

questions.question: If an individual with genotype Bb mates with an individual with genotype bb, what is the expected outcome in the offspring?
options: All the offspring will exhibit the dominant trait.
All the offspring will exhibit the recessive trait.
The recessive trait will show up in about 50% of the offspring.
The dominant trait will show up in about 75% of the offspring.
questions.explanation: Bb × bb produces 50% Bb (dominant) and 50% bb (recessive). Recessive trait appears in half the offspring.

questions.question: If an individual with genotype BB mates with an individual with genotype bb, what is the expected outcome in the offspring?
options: All offspring will exhibit the dominant trait.
All offspring will exhibit the recessive trait.
Half the offspring will show the recessive trait.
Three-fourths of the offspring will show the dominant trait.
questions.explanation: BB × bb produces all Bb offspring. All show dominant phenotype.

questions.question: If an individual with genotype bb mates with another individual with genotype bb, what is the expected outcome in the offspring?
options: All offspring will exhibit the dominant trait.
All offspring will exhibit the recessive trait.
Half the offspring will show the recessive trait.
Three-fourths of the offspring will show the dominant trait.
questions.explanation: bb × bb produces all bb offspring. All show recessive phenotype.

questions.question: If an individual with genotype Bb mates with another Bb individual, what is the expected outcome in the offspring?
options: All offspring will show the dominant trait.
Half the offspring will show the recessive trait.
One-fourth of the offspring will show the recessive trait.
Three-fourths of the offspring will show the recessive trait.
questions.explanation: Bb × Bb produces 1 BB : 2 Bb : 1 bb. Recessive phenotype (bb) appears in 1/4 of offspring.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered genotype analysis and probability calculations! 🧬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review gamete formation and monohybrid cross outcomes.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on dihybrid probabilities and DNA base pairing.
scoreMessages.0: Please review genotype notation, gamete calculation, and Mendelian cross results.
